implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-002 No fixed max-retry, documented in docstring
AC-004 Fresh correlation key per retry
AC-006 Additional-context propagation, no mutation of caller lists
AC-007 Style consistency with dispatch package

completion_promises.evidence: Created src/forge/dispatch/retry.py exporting class RetryCoordinator with async method retry_with_context(*, previous_outcome, capability, original_parameters, additional_context). Verified by tests TestRetryCoordinatorSurface.test_module_defines_retry_coordinator_class and test_retry_with_context_is_async (both passing).

completion_promises.criterion_text: No fixed max-retry count at this layer. The retry coordinator does NOT count attempts and does NOT refuse to retry. Document this in the module docstring.
completion_promises.evidence: Module docstring in src/forge/dispatch/retry.py opens with 'This module is intentionally policy-free. There is no fixed max-retry count at this layer (ASSUM-005)' and includes a 'Why no max-retry counter here' section explaining the reasoning loop owns retry policy. Implementation has no counter or refusal logic — every call forwards directly to orchestrator.dispatch(). Verified by TestNoFixedMaxRetryDocumented.test_module_docstring_documents_no_max_retry (passing) which asserts the docstring mentions both 'max-retry' and 'reasoning'.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Test (A.retry-with-additional-context): retry call's resulting DispatchOutcome carries attempt_no = previous_outcome.attempt_no + 1 and the new resolution record's retry_of field equals the previous resolution's resolution_id.
completion_promises.evidence: Implementation computes next_attempt_no = previous_outcome.attempt_no + 1 and passes retry_of=previous_outcome.resolution_id to orchestrator.dispatch(); the orchestrator then stamps retry_of onto the persisted CapabilityResolution copy. Verified by TestRetryWithAdditionalContext: test_retry_outcome_attempt_no_is_previous_plus_one (passes assertion retry.attempt_no == 2) and test_retry_persists_retry_of_link_to_previous_resolution (passes assertion exactly one persisted row carries retry_of=first.resolution_id).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Test (fresh correlation): the new dispatch attempt's correlation key is distinct from the previous attempt's. Verify via the registry's binding map.
completion_promises.evidence: RetryCoordinator does NOT pass through any correlation key — the orchestrator's CorrelationRegistry.fresh_correlation_key() issues a new 32-hex token per dispatch. Verified by TestFreshCorrelationKey.test_retry_uses_fresh_correlation_key_not_previous_one (passing): asserts publisher.published[0].attempt.correlation_key != publisher.published[1].attempt.correlation_key, that both match [0-9a-f]{32}, and that both keys appear exactly once in transport.subscribed_keys (the registry's binding-map projection).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Test (sibling not overwrite): after retry, BOTH resolution records exist in persistence — the original AND the sibling. The original is unchanged.
completion_promises.evidence: Verified by TestSiblingResolutionRecord.test_retry_creates_sibling_record_and_preserves_original (passing): asserts len(rows_after) == len(rows_before) + 1, asserts the original row equals its pre-retry snapshot via Pydantic structural equality, and asserts original.retry_of remains None. Also covered by the verbatim seam test TestSeamCapabilityResolutionSiblingContract.test_retry_creates_sibling_record_not_overwrite.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Test (additional context propagation): parameters list passed to orchestrator.dispatch() is original + additional_context in order; original parameters are not mutated.
completion_promises.evidence: Implementation uses list concatenation (original_parameters + additional_context), explicitly NOT in-place extend. Verified by TestAdditionalContextPropagation: test_orchestrator_receives_original_then_additional_in_order (asserts published params equal [("goal","ship"), ("repo","forge"), ("hint","check tests"), ("severity","high")] in exact order) and test_retry_does_not_mutate_caller_provided_lists (asserts id() and len() of caller-provided lists are unchanged after the call).

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: Both new files follow PEP 8, max line length 88, type-hinted, snake_case functions/PascalCase classes, full docstrings on module/class/method. No project lint runner is configured in pyproject.toml beyond pytest (no ruff/black/flake8 tooling declared). Code style mirrors the surrounding dispatch package (orchestrator.py, persistence.py) which is the de-facto style baseline.
